snp4.8:1.1: “‘Only here is there purity’ —that’s what they say—
snp4.8:1.2: ‘No other doctrines are pure’ —so they say.
snp4.8:1.3: Insisting that what they depend on is good,
snp4.8:1.4: they are deeply entrenched in idiosyncratic truths.
snp4.8:2.1: Seeking controversy, they plunge into an assembly,
snp4.8:2.3: Relying on others’ authority, they speak in debate.
snp4.8:2.4: Desiring praise, they claim to be skilled.
snp4.8:3.1: Engaged in disputes in the midst of the assembly,
snp4.8:3.4: Shaken with criticism, he seeks for an opening.
snp4.8:4.1: He whose doctrine is [judged as] demolished,
snp4.8:4.2: defeated, by those judging the issue:
snp4.8:4.3: He laments, he grieves—the inferior exponent.
snp4.8:5.1: These disputes have arisen among contemplatives.
snp4.8:5.3: Seeing this, one should abstain from disputes,
snp4.8:5.4: for they have no other goal than the gaining of praise.
snp4.8:6.1: While he who is praised there
snp4.8:6.2: for expounding his doctrine in the midst of the assembly,
snp4.8:6.3: laughs on that account & grows haughty,
snp4.8:7.1: That haughtiness will be grounds for his damage,
snp4.8:7.2: for he’ll speak in pride & conceit.
snp4.8:7.3: Seeing this, one should abstain from debates.
snp4.8:7.4: No purity is attained by them, say the skilled.
snp4.8:8.1: Like a strong man nourished on royal food,
snp4.8:8.2: you prowl about, roaring, searching out an opponent.
snp4.8:8.3: Wherever the battle is, go there, strong man.
snp4.8:9.1: Those who dispute, taking hold of a view,
snp4.8:9.2:
saying,
“This, and this only, is true,”
snp4.8:9.3:
those you can talk to.
Here there is nothing—
snp4.8:9.4:
no confrontation at the birth of disputes.
Whom would you gain as opponent, Pasūra,
snp4.8:10.1: among those who live above confrontation—
snp4.8:10.4: who have nothing here grasped as supreme?
snp4.8:11.3: You’re paired off with a pure one
